description: Serialization schema for a VTL model instance.

vtl-id.$comment: A VTL identifier, it should be the same as the VTL grammar

identifiable.$comment: Any VTL IM entity that can be identified with a VTL identifier

data.$comment: This schema section describes data such as datasets and scalars.

oneOf.$comment: A dataset is defined with the structure property, eventually specifying the components' domain

items.$comment: A dataset component overriding the structure component. Only needed when there's no reference to a represented variable, or the subset is a proper subset

subset.$comment: When overriding a represented variable, the subset must be compatible with its valuedomain

nullable.$comment: A flag specifying if the component subset allows null values. It should be false for identifiers and when the valuedomain of the represented variable does not allow null values.

oneOf.$comment: A scalar is defined by its value domain subset.

structures.$comment: This schema section describes data structures.

items.$comment: A datastructure is a identified collection of data structure components
A structure component is an identifiable, possibly referencing a represented variable, with a specific role

nullable.$comment: A flag specifying if the component allows null values. It must be false for identifiers.

variables.$comment: This schema section describes represented variables, such as age or price.

items.$comment: A VTL represented variable that may link, with a valuedomain, a data structure component to a dataset component

domains.$comment: This schema section describes valuedomains such as enumerated or described valuedomain.

oneOf.$comment: A VTL value domain externally specified. The meaning is implementation-dependent
A proper VTL valuedomain or valuedomain subset, restricting a parent valuedomain (subset).
An enumerated valuedomain or valuedomain subset. The enumeration items must be unique.

items.$comment: An enumeration item. It can be a code or an object containing a code with a description.

oneOf.$comment: A described value domain subset. The string should be a valid VTL expression describing it. It should not containing variables, except X as the value being validated.
